summary:
Cotton used after a formocresol pulpotomy must remain moist but not dripping wet; it serves both as an antimicrobial barrier while allowing some evaporation which aids tissue preservation without causing excessive dryness or necrosis.
The application method involves using pre-soaked gauze that remains just barely saturated rather than fully drenched so as neither promote bacterial growth nor lead to rapid dessication harming remaining viable tissues within tooth structure undergoing treatment via this minimally invasive approach targeting only coronal portions affected by decay leaving healthy root structures intact until further dental work can address them appropriately at later stages if needed based upon ongoing patient care needs assessment conducted regularly during follow up visits between now through completion phase when all necessary restorations have been performed successfully restoring full function back into compromised teeth thus ensuring long term health outcomes favorable enough warranting continued monitoring over time even beyond initial intervention itself due nature complexity involved requiring multiple steps sequential execution carefully planned out beforehand considering individual case
